claim_scope: Focused n=9 row-Ptolemy product-cancellation bookkeeping for the fixed natural cyclic order; not a proof of n=9, not a counterexample, not a geometric realizability test, and not a global status update.

filter.fixed_order_requirement: The row witness cyclic order must be supplied or certified; the selected-distance quotient alone is not an orderless abstract-incidence obstruction.
filter.name: row_ptolemy_product_cancel
filter.trigger: d02=d23 and d13=d01 for row witnesses w0,w1,w2,w3

certificates.contradiction: Ptolemy cancellation forces a product of two ordinary distances to be zero, but distinct strictly convex vertices have positive Euclidean distances.
certificates.scope: Exact obstruction for this fixed selected-witness row under the supplied/certified row order only.

certificates.scope: Exact obstruction for this fixed selected-witness row under the supplied/certified row order only.

interpretation: Each recorded hit is an exact obstruction for one fixed selected-witness assignment under the natural cyclic order.
The proof uses Ptolemy on a row witness circle plus exact selected-distance quotient equalities.
The filter kills 26 of the 184 n=9 pre-vertex-circle assignments, covering 3 of the 16 deterministic dihedral family labels.
For this n=9 frontier, every hit is also a vertex-circle self-edge case; this diagnostic does not dominate or replace the vertex-circle checker.
The row order must be supplied or certified. This is not an orderless abstract-incidence obstruction.
No proof of the n=9 case is claimed.

source_artifacts.role: deterministic family-id convention used for F02/F09/F13 labels
archived exploratory memo for the historical F15 alias

source_frontier.description: Complete n=9 assignments after pair/crossing/count filters and before vertex-circle obstruction.
